Advisor: Hi Mary, hi Joseph—good to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into the weeds, what prompted you to reach out now?
Client 1: Thanks. I’m Mary. We’re trying to sanity-check the whole picture—income, investments, taxes, and protection—so we’re not missing something obvious.
Client 2: Yeah, I’m Joseph. I mostly want it to feel simpler, honestly, but still on track.
Advisor: Totally fair. Let’s start with cash flow, then we’ll map the accounts and any insurance. Mary, what’s the household gross income per year right now—before taxes?

Advisor: Hi Mary, hi Joseph—good to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into the weeds, what prompted you to reach out now?
Client 1: Thanks. I’m Mary. We’re trying to sanity-check the whole picture—income, investments, taxes, and protection—so we’re not missing something obvious.
Client 2: Yeah, I’m Joseph. I mostly want it to feel simpler, honestly, but still on track.
Advisor: Totally fair. Let’s start with cash flow, then we’ll map the accounts and any insurance. Mary, what’s the household gross income per year right now—before taxes?

Advisor: Got it. And Mary, when we talk about taxes, which bracket do you generally think you’re in—top bracket, or do you know the band we should assume?
Client 1: Use 37%.
Advisor: Perfect—thank you. Last one is about how you want to work together, not a product thing. Are you looking for an RIA relationship—so, ongoing advice and management as a registered investment advisor—or more of a one-time plan?
Client 2: The ongoing one—RIA (registered investment advisor).
Advisor: Okay, that helps. And just so I’m not missing anything from earlier—income was Mary’s annual bonus and the interest and dividends, assets were the brokerage, retirement, joint cash, the small private markets piece, and the house; liabilities are still zero; and protection is the life policy. Sound right?

Advisor: Hi Mary, hi Joseph—good to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into the weeds, what prompted you to reach out now?

Advisor: Okay, last couple household items, Mary and Joseph—then we really are done for today.
Advisor: First, just to confirm your marital status for planning purposes—are you two married?
Client 1: Yes, we’re married.
Advisor: Great, thanks. Next: country—are we planning everything under US rules, or do either of you have a different home country for tax purposes?

Client 2: It’s US.
Advisor: Got it. And Mary, when we talk about taxes, which bracket do you generally think you’re in—top bracket, or do you know the band we should assume?
Client 1: Use 37%.

Client 2: The ongoing one—RIA (registered investment advisor).
